metadata.summary: In-loop evaluation tasks for language modeling

documentation:
# OLMo-in-loop-evals

Code for in-loop evaluation tasks used by the OLMo training team.

## Installation

```
pip install ai2-olmo-eval
```

classes.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.vocab_size
documentation: The tokenizer's vocabulary size.

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.pad_token_id
documentation: The tokenizer's padding token ID.

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.eos_token_id
documentation: The tokenizer's end-of-sentence token ID.

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.bos_token_id
documentation: The tokenizer's begin-of-sentence token ID.

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.encode
documentation:
Encode a string into token IDs.

```
param input

The string to encode.

param add_special_tokens

Include special tokens (like end-of-sentence tokens)
in the encoding.
```

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.encode_batch
documentation:
Encode a batch of inputs into token IDs.

```
param inputs

The strings to encode.

param add_special_tokens

Include special tokens (like end-of-sentence tokens)
in the encodings.
```

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.decode
documentation:
Decode a sequence of token IDs into a string.

```
param token_ids

The encoding to decode.

param skip_special_tokens

Exclude special tokens in the decoded string.
```

methods.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.Tokenizer.decode_batch
documentation:
Decode a batch of token IDs into a strings.

```
param batch_token_ids

The batch encoding to decode.

param skip_special_tokens

Exclude special tokens in the decoded strings.
```

An abstract base class for tokenizer's used by this library.

:::{tip}
Use the {class}`TokenizerConfig` to build tokenizer objects.
:::

classes.name: olmo_eval.tokenizer.HFTokenizer
documentation:
A {class}`Tokenizer` implementation that uses the [tokenizers](https://github.com/huggingface/tokenizers)
library from HuggingFace.

functions.name: olmo_eval.util.load_hf_dataset
documentation:
Loads a HuggingFace dataset. The dataset is assumed to be saved using
`save_hf_dataset_to_disk` and located in `olmo_data/hf_datasets`.

functions.name: olmo_eval.util.load_oe_eval_requests
documentation:
Loads an oe-eval request file from this package.
TODO: Add support from loading from S3 instead?

methods.name: olmo_eval.metrics.ICLMetric.__init__
documentation: metric_type: f1, acc, len_norm, pmi_dc, ce_loss, bpb

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset.prep_examples
documentation: Append doc_ids to each example so that they are processed together in the metric

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset.pad_tokens_until_max
documentation:
truncate from left if len(tokens) > model_ctx_len, max_len is not considered then
queries are already truncated at max length of model_ctx_len
this acts as additional check for all types of sequences in the batch

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset.doc_to_text
documentation:
Match EAI eval harness
returns a single context string

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset.doc_to_continuations
documentation:
Match EAI eval harness
returns a list of continuations

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset.doc_to_label
documentation:
Match EAI eval harness
returns continuation id which corresponds to true label

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ICLMultiChoiceTaskDataset.doc_to_domain_conditional
documentation:
Provide string for domain conditional normalization
by default its blank string, continuation normalized by prob conditioned on a blank
Only supports zero-shot for now.

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.PIQA
documentation:
PIQA sends context in the following fashion: "Question: GOAL
Answer:"
    space added as prefix to each continuation

    implement PMI_DC

    {
        'goal': "How do I ready a guinea pig cage for it's new occupants?",
        'sol1': 'Provide the guinea pig with a cage full of a few inches of bedding made of ripped paper strips, you will also need to supply it with a water bottle and a food dish.',
        'sol2': 'Provide the guinea pig with a cage full of a few inches of bedding made of ripped jeans material, you will also need to supply it with a water bottle and a food dish.',
        'label': 0
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.HellaSwag
documentation:
HellaSwag concats "ACTIVITY_LABEL: CTX_A CTX_B.capitalize()" to form context and then sends endings as continuations
    space added as prefix to each continuation

{
    'activity_label': 'Roof shingle removal',
    'ctx_a': 'A man is sitting on a roof.',
    'ctx_b': 'he',
    'ctx': 'A man is sitting on a roof. he',
    'endings': ['is using wrap to wrap a pair of skis.', 'is ripping level tiles off.', "is holding a rubik's cube.", 'starts pulling up roofing on a roof.'],
    'label': '3'
}

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.WinoGrande

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.WinoGrande.prep_examples
documentation: Overwrite for WinoGrande as multiple ctx, single continuation

methods.name: olmo_eval.tasks.WinoGrande.doc_to_domain_conditional
documentation: same number of domain conditionals as context

Prompt: split sentence at _ "SENTENCE[:idx] + OPTION1/OPTION2", where idx = SENTENCE.index("_")
    implement PMI_DC
    acc, random at 50%
    continuation is everything in setnence after '_' (" SENTENCE[idx:].strip()")

    Req_loglikelihood('People think Samantha', ' is embarassed, because Samantha made snide comments about the shirt Rebecca was wearing.')
    Req_loglikelihood('People think Rebecca', ' is embarassed, because Samantha made snide comments about the shirt Rebecca was wearing.')

{
    'sentence': 'People think _ is embarassed, because Samantha made snide comments about the shirt Rebecca was wearing.',
    'option1': 'Samantha',
    'option2': 'Rebecca',
    'answer': '2'
}

TODO: might need to write custom metric for Winogrande

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.OpenBookQA
documentation:
OBQA: question_stem is sent as context (no special prompt format) and choices are sent as continuation
    space added as prefix to each continuation

    implement PMI_DC

{
    'question_stem': 'Frilled sharks and angler fish live far beneath the surface of the ocean, which is why they are known as',
    'choices': {'text': ['Deep sea animals', 'fish', 'Long Sea Fish', 'Far Sea Animals'],
    'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']},
    'answerKey': 'A'
}

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.BoolQ
documentation:
Prompt: "PASSAGE
Question: QUESTION?
Answer:"
    acc, random at 50% (SuperGLUE)
    continuation: yes, no

    {
        'question': 'is ncis new orleans over for the season',
        'passage': 'NCIS: New Orleans (season 4) -- The fourth season of NCIS: New Orleans premiered on September 26, 2017 on CBS. The series continues to air following Bull, Tuesday at 10:00 p.m. (ET) and contained 24 episodes. The season concluded on May 15, 2018.',
        'label': 1
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.SciQ
documentation:
SciQ sends context as "SUPPORT
Question: QUESTION
Answer:" and then distractors + correct_answer as continuations
        space added as prefix to each continuation

        implement PMI_DC

    {
        'question': 'Who proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection?',
        'distractor3': 'Scopes',
        'distractor1': 'Linnaeus',
        'distractor2': 'shaw',
        'correct_answer': 'darwin',
        'support': ''
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ArcEasy
documentation:
ArcEasy creates context with "Question: QUESTION
Answer:" and sends the choices as continuations
        space added as prefix to each continuation

    {
        'question': 'Which technology was developed most recently?',
        'choices': {'text': ['cellular telephone', 'television', 'refrigerator', 'airplane'],
        'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']},
        'answerKey': 'A'
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ArcChallenge

class_variables.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ArcChallenge.metric_type
documentation: Ideally "pmi_dc"

ArcChallenge follows the same prompt format as ArcEasy.
implement PMI_DC

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.ArcEasyCELoss
documentation: ArcEasyCELoss is ARCEasy using an alternate ce_loss metric

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.BasicArithmetic
documentation:
This is a basic arithmetic task follows the same prompt format as ArcEasy.
Example:
{"id": "q85_1d1d_max1d_plus",
"question": "Calculate 2 + 5 =",
"choices": {"text": ["8", "7", "6", "17"],
"label": ["A", "B", "C", "D"]},
"answerKey": "B", "type_tag": "easy"}

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.CommonsenseQA
documentation:
CommonsenseQA
Example:
{'id': 'e68fb2448fd74e402aae9982aa76e527',
'question': 'Where are  you likely to find a hamburger?',
'question_concept': 'hamburger',
'choices': {'label': ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E'],
'text': ['fast food restaurant', 'pizza', 'ground up dead cows', 'mouth', 'cow carcus']},
'answerKey': 'A'}

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.SocialIQa
documentation:
SocialIQa
Example:
{'context': 'Jordan was in charge of taking the food on the camping trip and left all the food at home.',
 'question': 'How would Jordan feel afterwards?',
 'answerA': 'horrible that he let his friends down on the camping trip',
 'answerB': "happy that he doesn't need to do the cooking on the trip",
 'answerC': 'very proud and accomplished about the camping trip', 'label': '1'}

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.COPA
documentation:
Prompt: "PREMISE.strip()[:-1] because/therefore"
Req_loglikelihood('The pair of students came under scrutiny by the teacher because', ' the students both received excellent grades.'
continuations: CHOICE1/CHOICE2

"cause": "because",
"effect": "therefore",

implement PMI_DC
acc, random at 50%

{
    'premise': 'The pair of students came under scrutiny by the teacher.',
    'choice1': 'The students both received excellent grades.',
    'choice2': 'Their responses on the assignment were identical.',
    'question': 'cause',
    'label': 1
}

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.RTE
documentation:
Prompt: "SENTENCE1
Question: SENTENCE2 True or False?
Answer:"
    implement PMI_DC
    acc, random at 50% (GLUE)
    continuations: True, False

    {
        'sentence1': 'The number of Danes opposed to swapping the krone for the euro has increased slightly to 35.3 percent, up from 34.6 percent in April, according to a poll published on Thursday by Danske Bank.',
        'sentence2': 'The introduction of the euro has been opposed.',
        'label': 0,
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.CommitmentBank
documentation:
Prompt: "PREMISE
Question: HYPOTHESIS. True, False or Neither?
Answer:"
    continuations: True, False, Neither

        implement PMI_DC
        acc/F1, random at 33% acc. (SuperGLUE)

    {
        'premise': 'Then they would awake, terrified and sweating, to find themselves in white starched linen, in a comfortable bed, in peaceful England. And all would be well. It may be said that although he survived it the siege nevertheless had a bad effect on the Collector.',
        'hypothesis': 'the siege nevertheless had a bad effect on the Collector',
        'label': 0
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.MRPC
documentation:
Prompt for MRPC is formed using "Sentence 1: SENTENCE1
Sentence 2: SENTENCE2
Question: Do both sentences mean the same thing?
Answer:"
    acc/F1, random at 50% acc. (GLUE)
    continuations: yes and no

    {
        'sentence1': 'In fiction : Edward P. Jones ( " The Known World " ) and Scott Spencer ( " A Ship Made of Paper " ) .',
        'sentence2': 'The fifth nominee for fiction is Scott Spencer , for A Ship Made of Paper .',
        'label': 0
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.SST2
documentation:
SST2 task formats prompts as "SENTENCE
Question: Is this sentence positive or negative?
Answer:"
    some preprocessing done on sentence

    constructs 2 requests, 1 for positive and another for negative
    positive and negative have just 1 token in tokenizer
    positive: 1313
    negative: 2430

    implement PMI_DC
    acc, random at 50% (GLUE)

    {
        'sentence': "harrison 's flowers puts its heart in the right place , but its brains are in no particular place at all . ",
        'label': 1,
    }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.MMLU

class_variables.name: olmo_eval.tasks.MMLU.metric_type
documentation: Ideally pmi_dc

MMLU creates context with "Question: QUESTION
Answer:" and sends the choices as continuations
           space added as prefix to each continuation

       {
           'question': "Which of the following terms describes the body's ability to maintain its normal state?",
           'subject': 'anatomy',
           'choices': ['Anabolism', 'Catabolism', 'Tolerance', 'Homeostasis'],
    '       answer': 3
        }

classes.name: olmo_eval.tasks.TriviaQACELoss
olmo_eval.tasks.NaturalQuestionsCELoss
olmo_eval.tasks.OEEvalTask
documentation: Generic class for OE evaluation tasks
